Title: Parallel global profiling of plant TOR dynamics reveals a conserved role for LARP1 in translation

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes chlorophyll accumulation, normal seedling/root/leaf growth, and maintenance of TOR signaling; required for repression of 5'TOP mRNA translation upon TOR inhibition.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential for photosynthesis and chloroplast function.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Studied in growing seedlings under glucose-activated TOR conditions
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA mutant phenotyping; RNA-seq; Ribo-seq; proteomics; phosphoproteomics; chlorophyll quantification; amino acid profiling; S6K-pT449 immunoblot
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LARP1 is a TOR-sensitive phosphoprotein whose phosphorylation decreases after Torin2 treatment. 2. larp1 mutants show shorter roots, smaller leaves, reduced chlorophyll, and elevated alanine. 3. larp1 mutants display reduced TOR activity, evidenced by lower overlap with Torin2 phosphoproteomic signatures and reduced S6K-T449 phosphorylation. 4. LARP1 is required for TOR-dependent translational control of high-TOPscore 5'TOP mRNAs.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seedlings grown on half-strength MS under photosynthesis-limiting conditions; 15 mM glucose to activate TOR; 5.0 µM Torin2 for 2 h to attenuate TOR
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of LARP1 causes growth defects, chlorosis, reduced photosynthesis-associated gene/protein expression, and altered amino acid homeostasis (alanine accumulation).
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq and Ribo-seq: NCBI SRA PRJNA639161; Proteomics/phosphoproteomics: PRIDE PXD019942
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although central to TOR-dependent translational regulation, LARP1 is not an enzyme or transcription factor; it functions as a conserved RNA-binding effector of the TOR-LARP1-5'TOP axis. The paper’s main nutrient-metabolism causal gene focus is LARP1 rather than the many downstream 5'TOP target genes listed for context.

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: Ser/Thr protein kinase; master metabolic signaling kinase
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes ribosome biogenesis, translation, photosynthetic/chloroplast function, chlorophyll maintenance, and translation of 5'TOP mRNAs under glucose-sufficient conditions.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Chlorophyll supports light harvesting and photosynthesis; TOR also broadly coordinates anabolic metabolism.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Global metabolic regulation; ribosome biogenesis; mRNA translation; chloroplast translation; growth-related anabolism
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Kinase signaling that positively promotes translation and phosphorylation of downstream effectors; inhibition by Torin2 attenuates pathway output
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: LARP1; S6K/eS6b axis; eIF4B1; CBE1; RAPTOR1; translational control of 5'TOP mRNAs
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes translation of ribosomal protein mRNAs and high-TOPscore 5'TOP transcripts; supports expression of photosynthesis-related functions
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Possible autoregulatory feedback within TORC1 suggested by TOR-sensitive phosphorylation of RAPTOR1; LARP1 is required to maintain high TOR activity
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Acts in TORC1 with RAPTOR1 and LST8
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Assayed in glucose-activated seedlings and after acute Torin2 inhibition
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Torin2 inhibition; RNA-seq; Ribo-seq; proteomics; phosphoproteomics; chlorophyll assay; phospho-S6K immunoblot
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Acute TOR inhibition broadly represses ribosomal protein mRNAs and translational efficiency of translation-related and chloroplast-associated transcripts. 2. TOR-sensitive phosphoproteomics identified multiple downstream targets including eS6b, LARP1, eIF4B1, CBE1, RAPTOR1, TOPLESS, VLN2, and VLN3. 3. TOR specifically promotes translation of high-TOPscore 5'TOP mRNAs through a LARP1-dependent mechanism. 4. TOR activity supports chlorophyll accumulation and chloroplast physiology.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 15 mM glucose activates TOR; 5.0 µM Torin2 for 2 h attenuates TOR in seedlings under photosynthesis-limiting conditions
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: TOR inhibition represses growth, ribosome biogenesis, photosynthesis/chlorophyll, and induces stress/catabolic programs.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Master regulator coordinating nutrient status with translation and chloroplast-associated anabolic output.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq and Ribo-seq: NCBI SRA PRJNA639161; Proteomics/phosphoproteomics: PRIDE PXD019942
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: TOR is the upstream signaling focus of the study, but the paper’s experimentally dissected core causal downstream gene for nutrient metabolism-related output is LARP1.
